title: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. (NCLH) CEO Frank Del Rio on Q2 2022 Results - Earnings Call Transcript
text: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. (NYSE:NCLH ) Q2 2022 Results Conference Call August 9, 2022 10:00 AM ET Company Participants Jessica John - VP, IR, ESG and Corporate Communications Frank Del Rio - President and CEO Mark Kempa - EVP and CFO Conference Call Participants Dan Politzer - Wells Fargo Brandt Montour - Barclays Steve Wieczynski - Stifel Patrick Scholes - Truist Vince Ciepiel - Cleveland Research Robin Farley - UBS Jamie Katz - Morningstar Operator Good morning, and welcome to the Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Business Update and Second Quarter 2022 Earnings Conference Call.

title: Why Is Norwegian Cruise (NCLH) Stock Down 12% Today?
text: Source: Nazar Skladanyi / Shutterstock Norwegian Cruise (NYSE: NCLH ) stock is sinking on Tuesday as investors react to the company's earnings report for the second quarter of 2022. In that earnings report, Norwegian Cruise posts adjusted earnings per share of -$1.14.

title: Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) Reports Q2 Loss, Misses Revenue Estimates
text: Norwegian Cruise Line (NCLH) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of -39.02% and 6.09%, respectively, for the quarter ended June 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: Norwegian Cruise Line posts wider-than-expected loss and misses revenue target
text: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. NCLH, +2.42% fell 8.8% in premarket trades Tuesday after it warned it'll post a third-quarter loss and fall short of revenue targets.

title: Norwegian Cruise misses revenue estimates on occupancy constraints
text: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd reported quarterly revenue below Wall Street estimates on Tuesday as U.S. cruise liners resuming operations after more than a year recorded weaker occupancy levels.

title: Could Smaller be Better for Investors in Norwegian Cruise Lines?
text: Is it time for investors to take the plunge on Norwegian Cruise Lines stock? The answer may come from the company's decision to buck the industry trend
